title: Use of dates
excerpt:
How do you all use start date, due date and/or timeline? 
Whats the difference in the use of these columns? 
Do you use all of these at the same time, or just one?

posts.author: Pierce007 (Rune Haarberg)
posts.cookedHtml:
How do you all use start date, due date and/or timeline?
Whats the difference in the use of these columns?
Do you use all of these at the same time, or just one?

posts.author: Fario_Consulting (Fario Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,
You could create a Timeline column type where you input the start and due dates
Cheers,
Harsh

posts.author: sirah (Sirah E)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi Rune!

Sirah here from Mindflows!

A date column is can be used in many uses cases such as start date or expected due date and much more depends on your use case

A timeline column is useful for you and your team to understand how long you have to complete an item

To know more you can check out Monday’s Knowledge base about the timeline column here

Hope this helps!
